displayTitle: Turning an Invisible Impedance Shift Into a Signal
kicker: ET LEVEL I // IMPEDANCE TO INSTRUMENT

beats.narration: The part shifts your coil's impedance, but the change is invisible — for a fine crack, often under one percent of the total.

payload.title: IMPEDANCE TO SIGNAL
payload.sub: A change too small to see, turned into a moving dot.

beats.narration: Ohm's law for AC carries it. Hold the drive current constant, and voltage across the coil equals current times impedance.

payload.sub: Constant current makes coil voltage a faithful copy of impedance.

beats.narration: Every instrument runs the same chain. Oscillator drives the coil; balance nulls the standing voltage; the amplifier boosts the tiny difference; the phase detector splits it.

payload.heading: The signal chain every instrument shares
payload.note: OSCILLATOR → BALANCE → AMPLIFIER → PHASE DETECTOR → DISPLAY

beats.narration: The phase detector resolves the signal into horizontal and vertical. That pair moves one dot, carrying two channels at once.
Same setup, same signal — so signals become evidence. Amplitude tells you how big; phase tells you what kind, unlocking the impedance plane next.

payload.sub: The pair of ideas that unlocks the impedance plane.
